---
title: "Huila — Lagged Effects: Education & Suicide Mortality"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Setup
```{r}
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(jsonlite)
library(stringi)
```

## Load and clean suicide data
```{r}
suicide <- read_csv(here("../../outputs/csv/suicide_huila.csv")) |>
  filter(
    sexo == "Total",
    !territorio %in% c("Huila", "Nacional")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    territorio = stri_trans_general(territorio, "Latin-ASCII"),
    territorio = trimws(tolower(territorio))
  ) |>
  select(territorio, anio, valor)
```

## Download full education time series for all Huila municipalities
```{r}
#| cache: true

descargar_socrata <- function(base_url, batch_size = 50000, extra_query = NULL) {
  offset <- 0
  batches <- list()
  i <- 1
  repeat {
    url <- paste0(
      base_url,
      "?$limit=", batch_size,
      "&$offset=", offset,
      if (!is.null(extra_query)) paste0("&", extra_query) else ""
    )
    batch <- fromJSON(url, flatten = TRUE)
    if (nrow(batch) == 0) break
    batches[[i]] <- batch
    if (nrow(batch) < batch_size) break
    offset <- offset + batch_size
    i <- i + 1
  }
  bind_rows(batches)
}

select_fields <- paste(c(
  "a_o", "municipio",
  "cobertura_bruta", "cobertura_neta",
  "deserci_n", "aprobaci_n", "reprobaci_n", "repitencia"
), collapse = ",")

where_clause <- URLencode("departamento = 'Huila'", reserved = TRUE)
extra_query <- paste0("$select=", select_fields, "&$where=", where_clause)

edu_raw <- descargar_socrata(
  "https://www.datos.gov.co/resource/nudc-7mev.json",
  extra_query = extra_query
)
```

## Clean and join
```{r}
edu <- edu_raw |>
  mutate(
    anio            = as.integer(a_o),
    cobertura_bruta = as.numeric(cobertura_bruta),
    cobertura_neta  = as.numeric(cobertura_neta),
    desercion       = as.numeric(deserci_n),
    aprobacion      = as.numeric(aprobaci_n),
    reprobacion     = as.numeric(reprobaci_n),
    repitencia      = as.numeric(repitencia),
    territorio      = stri_trans_general(municipio, "Latin-ASCII"),
    territorio      = trimws(tolower(territorio))
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(anio)) |>
  select(
    territorio, anio, cobertura_bruta, cobertura_neta,
    desercion, aprobacion, reprobacion, repitencia
  )

# Join — only years where both datasets have data
df <- suicide |>
  inner_join(edu, by = c("territorio", "anio")) |>
  filter(anio >= 2011, anio <= 2024) |>
  arrange(territorio, anio)

# Quick check
df |>
  count(territorio) |>
  summarise(
    n_municipios = n(),
    min_obs = min(n),
    max_obs = max(n)
  )
```

## Cross-correlation across all municipalities and lags
```{r}
edu_vars <- c(
  "desercion", "cobertura_bruta", "cobertura_neta",
  "aprobacion", "reprobacion", "repitencia"
)

lags <- 0:5

# For each municipality, compute lagged correlations
# then average across municipalities
panel_correlations <- map_dfr(edu_vars, function(var) {
  map_dfr(lags, function(lag) {
    # Compute correlation per municipality
    muni_cors <- df |>
      group_by(territorio) |>
      arrange(anio) |>
      summarise(
        r = {
          x <- get(var)
          y <- valor
          n <- length(x)
          if (lag > 0 && n > lag + 2) {
            cor(x[1:(n - lag)], y[(lag + 1):n],
              use = "complete.obs",
              method = "spearman"
            )
          } else if (lag == 0 && n > 2) {
            cor(x, y,
              use = "complete.obs",
              method = "spearman"
            )
          } else {
            NA_real_
          }
        },
        .groups = "drop"
      ) |>
      filter(!is.na(r))

    tibble(
      indicator  = var,
      lag        = lag,
      mean_r     = mean(muni_cors$r),
      median_r   = median(muni_cors$r),
      sd_r       = sd(muni_cors$r),
      n_munis    = nrow(muni_cors)
    )
  })
})

panel_correlations
```

## Visualise — average correlation across municipalities by lag
```{r}
#| fig-height: 8

panel_correlations |>
  ggplot(aes(x = lag, y = mean_r, fill = mean_r > 0)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean_r - sd_r, ymax = mean_r + sd_r),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~indicator, ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = lags) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#d7191c", "#2c7bb6"), guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Huila — Education indicators vs suicide mortality",
    subtitle = "Mean Spearman r across 37 municipalities ± SD | lags 0–5 years",
    x = "Lag (years)",
    y = "Mean Spearman r"
  )
```

## Distribution of municipality-level correlations at strongest lag
```{r}
# Find strongest lag per indicator from panel results
strongest_lags <- panel_correlations |>
  group_by(indicator) |>
  slice_max(abs(mean_r), n = 1) |>
  select(indicator, lag)

# Recompute municipality-level correlations at those lags
muni_level <- map_dfr(edu_vars, function(var) {
  best_lag <- strongest_lags |>
    filter(indicator == var) |>
    pull(lag)

  df |>
    group_by(territorio) |>
    arrange(anio) |>
    summarise(
      r = {
        x <- get(var)
        y <- valor
        n <- length(x)
        if (best_lag > 0 && n > best_lag + 2) {
          cor(x[1:(n - best_lag)], y[(best_lag + 1):n],
            use = "complete.obs",
            method = "spearman"
          )
        } else {
          cor(x, y, use = "complete.obs", method = "spearman")
        }
      },
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    mutate(indicator = var, lag = best_lag)
})

# Plot distribution
muni_level |>
  ggplot(aes(x = r, fill = indicator)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 10, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~indicator, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of municipality-level correlations at strongest lag",
    subtitle = "Each bar = one municipality in Huila",
    x = "Spearman r",
    y = "Number of municipalities"
  )
```
